LAW AND MOTION, 302, AS TO DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY SAN JUAN MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRERS TO VERIFIED COMPLAINT, DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL REPORTS THERE IS A PENDING CCP 170.6 PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE AS TO THE HON. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS. THE COURT GRANTS THE CHALLENGE. THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUES THE MATTER TO AUGUST 30, 2017 AT 9:30 AM TO BE HEARD BY THE HON. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Lindsey Barnhart of the law offices of Covington & Burling LLP (415-591-7034) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Amanda Goad of the American Civil Liberties Union of Southern California (213-977-5218) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorneys, Harvey L. Rochman and Craig S.Rutenberg of the law offices of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips (310-312-4104) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party

Judge Kahn discloses that one of the attorneys for Mr. Minton is the sister of a person whom Judge Kahn considers to be part of his extended family, but Judge Kahn believes that that fact does not cause him to be other than fair or impartial in this case or otherwise require his recusal. The precise relationship is that Elizabeth Gill is the sister of the brother-in-law of Judge Kahn's nephew. Counsel stipulate and the hearing proceeds.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with minor modification.

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. As was true with Mr. Minton's complaint, the first amended complaint fails to allege sufficient facts to show that Dignity Health's conduct in permitting Mr. Minton to receive a hysterectomy at one of its hospitals other than the hospital where Mr. Minton desired to receive that procedure violated Dignity Health's obligation per Civil Code 51(b) to provide full and equal access to medical procedures without regard to gender. (North Coast Women's Care Medical Group, Inc. v. San Diego County Superior Court (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 1145, 1159). Because it appears to be considered of import by the Supreme Court and part of the teachings of that case, there is no basis for this court to ignore the dicta in North Coast. As applied to Mr. Minton's sole claim for violation of the Unruh Act, the North Coast dicta shows that the claim is not viable. Mr. Minton has not alleged, nor does it appear that it is reasonably possible for him to allege, that his receiving the procedure he desired from the physician he selected to perform that procedure three days later than he had planned and at a different hospital than he desired deprived him of full and equal access to the procedure, even assuming, as the court is required to do on demurrer, that Dignity Health's refusal to have the procedure performed at MSJMC was substantially motivated by Mr. Minton's gender identity. Both parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Lewis Gillian, CSR No. 12355, e-mail: lgillian@sftc.org. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Continued To Jan-03-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Nov-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Theodore Karch, Esq. (415-591-7094) of Covington & Burling LLP for Plaintiff/ opposing party
Amanda Goad, Esq. (213-977-5218) of ACLU for Plaintiff/Opposing party
Barry Landsberg, Esq. and Harvey Rochman, Esq. (310-312-4104/4259) of Manatt Phelps & Phillips LLP for Defendant/Moving party.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the basis for the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Argues. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY SAN JUAN MEDICAL CENTER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Dignity Health's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Minton is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Although Mr. Minton's complaint is silent about the reason why his request for a hysterectomy at MSJMC was denied, both sides agree that the reason was MSJMC's interpretation of the Ethical and Religious Directives for Catholic Health Care Services. Based on this agreement and the parties' memoranda having addressed the religion-based arguments raised by Dignity Health, the court has treated those arguments as properly raised on this demurrer. In paragraphs 23-26 of his complaint, Mr. Minton has alleged insufficient facts to show that Dignity Health's conduct in permitting Mr. Minton to receive a hysterectomy at another of its hospitals violated Dignity Health's obligation per Civil Code 51(b) to provide "full and equal" access to medical procedures without regard to gender. (North Coast Women's Care Medical Group, Inc. v. San Diego County Superior Court (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 1145, 1159). While the dicta in North Coast involved a medical procedure performed by a different doctor in the same facility, that dicta is, at least in some situations, also applicable to a medical procedure performed by the same doctor in a different facility. Because it is at least theoretically possible that Mr. Minton is able to allege sufficient facts to avoid a demurrer based on the North Coast dicta, if he can do so in good faith, he is given leave to amend to allege such facts. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Order is signed. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
